sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 15.55 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.15)

statement.latex: a) The Monster,$M$, is a$6$-trans\-po\-si\-tion group. Pairs of Fischer transpositions generate 9 \$M$-classes of dihedral groups. The order of the product of a pair is the coefficient of the highest root of affine type$E_8$. Similar properties hold for Baby$B$, and$F_{24}'$with respect to$E_7$and$E_6$when the product is read modulo centres ($2.B$,$3.F_{24}'$). Explain this. \ Editors' Comment of ...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
